items.title: Label visualizations honestly
items.avoid: Showing a 2D plot without stating which parameters vary and which are fixed.
items.prefer: Caption slices as 'L(w₁, w₂) with other θ fixed at θ*' or 'random 2D projection from θ*'.
items.why: Honest labeling prevents over-interpreting partial views as the full hypersurface.
items.example: Title: 'Loss contour over output-layer weights only (hidden layers frozen at epoch-10 values)'.

items.title: Monitor both path and surroundings
items.avoid: Watching only the training loss curve and ignoring gradient norm and parameter movement.
items.prefer: Track loss, gradient norm ||∇L||, and optionally a local 1D line search to detect plateaus or instability.
items.why: The curve is altitude on one path; gradient norm signals whether you are on a cliff, floor, or flat plain nearby.
items.example: Loss flat but ||∇L|| ≈ 1e-4 for many steps → plateau; try higher LR or warmup instead of more epochs.

items.title: Compare runs in landscape terms
items.avoid: Declaring one model better solely by final training loss.
items.prefer: Compare validation metrics, loss sensitivity to small weight noise, and seeds when diagnosing generalization.
items.why: Different valleys can share similar depth but different width; width often correlates with robustness.
items.example: Add Gaussian noise σ=0.01 to weights: if validation loss spikes 3× for run A but 10% for run B, B likely sits in a flatter basin.
